prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, in this most mystifying epoch, the coffers of yonder cloud-based concern didst swell with an influx of 437,000 novel patrons, a sum greater than the entire population of our fair realm. 'Tis said that the mystical "revenue" didst increase by a factor of 32% over the preceding quarter, a growth so fantastical that mine own quill doth tremble at the very notion. The wisest sages of the company didst aver that this bounty was wrought by the "expansion of their sales team" and the "enhancement of their product offerings", incantations whose meaning escapeth mine understanding. In a ritual of great import, the chief sorcerer of the firm didst proclaim unto the gathered throng that the "customer acquisition costs" hadst risen by 17%, a portent whose significance mine own befuddled brain doth struggle to divine.
